committees: [{'name': 'House Natural Resources', 'systemCode': 'hsii00', 'url': 'https://api.congress.gov/v3/committee/house/hsii00?format=json'}]
title:
The Committee on Natural Resources has scheduled a markup beginning on Tuesday, May 24, 2016 at 5:00 p.m.  The Committee will consider the following bill: 

•	H.R. 5278 (Rep. Sean Duffy), To establish an Oversight Board to assist the Government of Puerto Rico, including instrumentalities, in managing its public finances, and for other purposes. ‘‘Puerto Rico Oversight, Management, and Economic Stability Act” or “PROMESA”.

	On Tuesday, May 24, 2016, the Committee will convene at 5:00 p.m. in 1324 Longworth House Office Building for opening statements only.  The Committee will reconvene on Wednesday, May 25, 2016 at 10:00 a.m. until 12:30 p.m. in the 1324 Longworth House Office Building.

committees: [{'name': 'House Natural Resources', 'systemCode': 'hsii00', 'url': 'https://api.congress.gov/v3/committee/house/hsii00?format=json'}]
title:
Markup beginning on Wednesday September 9, 2015 at 4:00 p.m.  
The Committee will consider the following bills: 

• H.R. 538 (Rep. Don Young), To facilitate the development of energy on Indian lands by reducing Federal regulations that impede tribal development of Indian lands, and for other purposes.  “Native American Energy Act”
• H.R. 1541 (Rep. Raul Grijalva), To amend title 54, United States Code, to make Hispanic-serving institutions eligible for technical and financial assistance for the establishment of preservation training and degree programs.  “PRISM Act”
• H.R. 1644 (Rep. Alexander Mooney), To amend the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 to ensure transparency in the development of environmental regulations, and for other purposes.  “STREAM Act”
• H.R. 1880 (Rep. Michelle Lujan Grisham), To require the Secretary of the Interior to take into trust 4 parcels of Federal land for the benefit of certain Indian Pueblos in the State of New Mexico.  “Albuquerque Indian School Land Transfer Act” 
• H.R. 2130 (Rep. Mac Thornberry), To provide legal certainty to property owners along the Red River in Texas, and for other purposes.  “Red River Private Property Protection Act”
• H.R. 2168 (Rep. Jamie Herrera Beutler), To make the current Dungeness crab fishery management regime permanent and for other purposes.  “West Coast Dungeness Crab Management Act”
•	H.R. 2288 (Rep. Bob Goodlatte), To remove the use restrictions on certain land transferred to Rockingham County, Virginia, and for other purposes.  

On Wednesday, September 9, 2015, the Committee will convene at 4:00 p.m. in 1324 Longworth House Office Building for opening statements only.  The Committee will reconvene on Thursday, September 10, 2015 at 10:00 a.m. until 12:00 p.m. in the 1324 Longworth House Office Building.

title:
The Committee on Natural Resources has scheduled a markup on: 
• H.R. 487 (Rep. Markwayne Mullin), To allow the Miami Tribe of Oklahoma to lease or transfer certain lands.
•	H.R. 959 (Rep. Bennie Thompson), To authorize the Secretary of the Interior to conduct a special resource study of the Medgar Evers House, located in Jackson, Mississippi, and for other purposes.  “Medgar Evers House Study Act”
• H.R. 1554 (Rep. Scott Tipton), To require a land conveyance involving the Elkhorn Ranch and the White River National Forest in the State of Colorado, and for other purposes. “Elkhorn Ranch and White River National Forest Conveyance Act of 2015” 
•	H.R. 1937 (Rep. Mark Amodei), To require the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Agriculture to more efficiently develop domestic sources of the minerals and mineral materials of strategic and critical importance to United States economic and national security and manufacturing competitiveness. "National Strategic and Critical Minerals Production Act of 2015" 
• H.R.1949 (Rep. G.K. Butterfield), To provide for the consideration and submission of site and design proposals for the National Liberty Memorial approved for establishment in the District of Columbia. “The National Liberty Memorial Clarification Act of 2015”
• H.R. 2223 (Rep. Doug Lamborn), To authorize, direct, expedite, and facilitate a land exchange in El Paso and Teller Counties, Colorado, and for other purposes. “Crags, Colorado Land Exchange Act of 2015” 
• H.R. 2791 (Rep. Peter DeFazio), To require that certain Federal lands be held in trust by the United States for the benefit of certain Indian tribes in Oregon, and for other purposes.  “Western Oregon Tribal Fairness Act”
• H.R. 2898 (Rep. David Valadao), To provide drought relief in the State of California, and for other purposes.  “Western Water and American Food Security Act of 2015”
• S. 501 (Sen. Tom Udall), To make technical corrections to the Navajo water rights settlement in the State of New Mexico, and for other purposes. “New Mexico Water Settlement Technical Corrections Act”


On Wednesday, July 8, 2015, the Committee will convene at 4:00 p.m. in 1324 Longworth House Office Building for opening statements only.  The Committee will reconvene on Thursday, July 9, at 10:00 a.m. until 12:30 p.m. in the 1324 Longworth House Office Building.
